$comment: Real output from: node client/exec-bash.ts "ip-info | jq -r .country"
$why_this_matters: When the pipeline ends in a non-registry transform (jq, grep, awk), the final stdout shape doesn't match any skill's outputSchema — schema_check would false-positive. Smart-bash detects this and explains: 'compare against the upstream tool's example, not the schema'.

schema_check.reason: Pipeline ends in a transform (jq -r .country), not a registry tool — final stdout shape is up to your post-processing. Compare to the example output of the upstream registry tool listed in tools_referenced.

$delta: Smart still emits the upstream tool's schema for reference, but the schema_check.reason explicitly tells the model the validation was skipped because of post-processing. The model can compare its expected stdout (`MX`) against the upstream example (`country: '<ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code>'`) to verify it extracted the right field.
